title: Grangea domingensis
taxonomy: Grangea domingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Heliantheae, which is a group of daisies and related plants. The genus Grangea is a small group of plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. Grangea domingensis is native to the island of Hispaniola, which is shared by Haiti and the Dominican Republic. This plant is often classified as a shrub or a subshrub due to its woody base and branching stems.
